summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Chest pain

History of Present Illness
The patient reports chest pain, describing it as aching and radiating to their neck. He states the pain started approximately one hour ago and has been worsening. He denies any recent trauma. He reports anxiety and describes feeling heavy. The patient also reports difficulty stopping pooping and has been experiencing some elbow pain. He has a past medical history of hypertension and diabetes. He currently takes metformin 500mg daily and norvasc 2.5mg daily, and also takes motrin for the pain.

Findings
The patient's blood pressure was elevated. An EKG will be ordered. The patient reports pain in their left elbow and arm. The patient reports a feeling of heaviness.

Assessment
The patient is concerned about a possible heart attack. The patient's blood pressure is elevated. The patient has diabetes.

Plan
An EKG will be ordered. Aspirin will be administered. Nitroglycerin will be administered. The doctor orders a blood pressure cuff for the patient. Follow up in four weeks if symptoms persist.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Left arm pain.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Zachary Wood is a 45-year-old right-hand-dominant male who presents to the clinic today for the evaluation of left arm pain. The onset of his pain began yesterday when he woke up with pain. He denies any specific injury. The patient locates his pain from his left elbow up all the way to his neck. He describes his pain as an achiness. The patient has been taking Motrin for pain, which does not provide him with relief. He also reports intermittent chest pain that radiates up his neck. He notes that he had to stop cutting the grass the other day because of chest pain. The patient denies any shortness of breath.

For his hypertension, the patient states that his blood pressure has been around 125/72 recently. He is still taking 2.5 mg of Norvasc.

Regarding his type 2 diabetes, his last hemoglobin A1c was 7.2. He takes metformin 500 mg. The patient states that he does not check his blood glucose levels regularly.

MEDICAL HISTORY

Patient reports that he has a past medical history of hypertension and type 2 diabetes.

MEDICATIONS

Patient reports that he currently takes metformin 500 mg and Norvasc 2.5 mg.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Cardiovascular: Reports chest pain.
Respiratory: Denies shortness of breath.
Musculoskeletal: Reports left elbow, arm, and neck pain.

VITALS

The patient's blood pressure today is 150/90.

PHYSICAL EXAM

CV: Slight murmur detected, not heard before.

ASSESSMENT

Left arm pain.
Type 2 diabetes.
Hypertension.

PLAN

Regarding his left arm pain, I have explained to him that his symptoms are likely a mild heart attack. I have recommended that we obtain an EKG for further evaluation. I have also recommended that the patient take aspirin and nitroglycerin for pain. The patient will follow up with me once the EKG results are available for review and further discussion.

In regards to his diabetes, the patient will continue the 500 mg of metformin for now.

For his hypertension, the patient will continue the 2.5 mg of Norvasc.

Chief Complaint
Chest pain

The patient reports chest pain, describing it as aching and radiating to their neck. He states the pain started approximately one hour ago and has been worsening. He denies any recent trauma. section_miscategorizations.reason: Ordering an EKG is a planned diagnostic order, not an existing exam or test result.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports pain in their left elbow and arm.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a patient-reported symptom and belongs in the history rather than objective findings.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports a feeling of heaviness.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a subjective symptom description and should be part of the HPI.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is concerned about a possible heart attack.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is the patient's expressed concern, a subjective history element, not the clinician's diagnostic impression.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's blood pressure is elevated.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an objective vital sign finding; while often referenced in assessment, its primary placement should be under findings.
